titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD45/13/259

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=246 status=live modified=2021-04-04 14:41:07.881388+00 | translation moa=247 status=live modified=2021-04-04 14:41:10.56136+00

div.p:
A . dei gratia . Rex Scott’ . vicecomiti suo ⁊ balliuis suis de Dumfres’ salutem. Sciatis quod inspeximus cartas illustrium Regum
Dauid ⁊ Malcolmi antecessorum nostrorum . ⁊ domini Willelmi regis patris nostri . in quibus continetur . quod abbas ⁊ Conuentus de Sancta
Cruce curiam liberam habere debent tam in terra de Dunrod’ quam tenent in Galuuath’ de dono Fergus ⁊ heredum suorum .
quam in aliis terris quas habent de dono antecessorum nostrorum . scilicet in Brochtun’ ⁊ hameram . vnde uobis mandamus ⁊
firmiter precipimus . quatinus nullo modo permittatis ut aliquis heredum Galuuath’ . aut eorum balliui . dictos abbatem ⁊ conuentum
impediant . quin Curiam suam liberam habeant in dicta terra de Dunrod’ de omnibus que ad ipsos iuste debent pertinere .
Tantum facientes . ne pro defectu vestri  oporteat dictos abbatem et conuentum . ad nos super hoc amplius recurrere conquerendo.
Testibus Henrico . de Balliol’ Camerario . Iohanne de Vallibus . Nicholao de Sulis’ apud Edinburc xvi . die februarii . Anno regni domini regis . xxx . secundo .

Alexander by the grace of God king of Scots to his sheriff and bailies of Dumfries Greeting: Know that we have inspected the charters of the renowned kings David and Malcolm our ancestors, and of the lord King William our father, in which it is contained that the abbot and covnent of Holyrood ought so to have free court in the land of Dunrod – which they hold in Galloway of the gift of Fergus and his heirs – as in the other lands that they have of the gift of our ancestors, that is, in Broughton and Hamera [now Whitekirk]. Wherefore we command you and firmly enjoin, that you should by no means permit that any of the heirs of Galloway, or their bailies, obstruct the said abbot and convent, that they may rather have their free court in the said land of Dunrod respecting everything that ought justly to pertain to them; doing as much lest because of your failure it be necessary for the said abbot and convent to return to us once more to complain about this matter. As witnesses, Henry de Balliol, chamberlain, John de Vaux, Nicholas de Soulis, at Edinburgh on the 16th day of February, in the thirty-second year of the reign of the lord king.
